title: Phosphate-buffered Saline (PBS)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dkre8","text":"This protocol describes how to make 1 L of PBS to a final concentration of 1x or 10x solution. The solution can be made RNase free with the addition of DEPC (diethyl pyrocarbonate) as in step 5.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: For a final concentration of 1x PBS weigh out the following amount of each reagent: NaCl- 8g, KCl- 0.2 g, Na₂HPO4- 1.44 g, KH₂PO4- 0.24 g. For a final concentration of 10x PBS weigh out the following amount of each reagent: NaCl- 80g, KCl- 2 g, Na₂HPO4- 14.4 g, KH₂PO4- 2.4 g.
Dissolve reagents in 800 mL of triple distilled H₂O using a magnetic stirrer and flea or by manual shaking.
Using a pH meter, adjust pH of solution to 7.4 (or 7.2 if required) by adding concentrated hydrochloric acid (HCl).
Add triple distilled H₂O to bring final volume of solution to 1 L
If require RNase free PBS, add 1 mL of DEPC (diethyl pyrocarbonate) per 1 L of PBS.
creator: Katy M Monteith
